text.title: OpenAPI Spec Authoring Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-openapi-spec work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Spec has 40 unused $refs, 3 inconsistent path styles, 2 typos in security scheme names.
antipattern.root-cause: Spectral never run; or run with all rules disabled.
antipattern.fix: Adopt templates/spectral.yaml; gate CI on zero errors. See rule spectral-clean.

antipattern.symptom: User object inlined in 18 endpoints; adding an `is_verified` field requires 18 edits + 1 inevitable miss.
antipattern.root-cause: Schemas inlined for brevity; never extracted.
antipattern.fix: Pull each repeated schema into components.schemas; replace inlines with $ref. See rule no-inline-duplicate-schema.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint documents 200 response as `{ "data": {} }`; generated SDK types are `any`; clients write defensive parsers.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped schema work for speed.
antipattern.fix: Force every documented response to have a typed schema; reject `{}` responses in CI. See rule strict-response-schemas.

antipattern.symptom: Docs say JWT Bearer; runtime accepts API key in `X-Api-Key` only; partner integration delayed by half a sprint.
antipattern.fix: Cross-check securitySchemes against AUTH-* on every spec PR; fail mismatch. See rule security-block-aligned.
